textByHashes.5df24c495ab2d5eee1b1221e1a33956c2be6d164: We're making progress on those plagues. With our citizens back, we had enough people to finally drive out the frogs and locusts that were filling the town up.
textByHashes.0fdf4a40bce434ee2a75bd4f079995c116329865: We worried they'd just come back, but perhaps we have managed to appease whichever god sent them.
textByHashes.1110a908662519fd1df14431422d4126df1666b3: The infected cows have been dealt with as well. The only plague left now is this infuriating pox. Still, at least it isn't contagious.
textByHashes.d928f848a2d0e29d4c979ff056dba9d82a8c8625: Free of the plagues? How is this possible? We tried everything!
textByHashes.6711ba91b323919153a9a1b572c0005cf2bd157d: Er... mostly I prayed to Icthlarin and hoped for the best.
textByHashes.02a3fe9336b719ede5e14b2c2ab768b34de4e53f: Thank you, friend. You are once again our saviour!
textByHashes.1ff0484164738a18e77cd7dae94f043c5c2dd1cb: Thank you for bringing us that cure. We will hopefully start seeing the effects soon.
textByHashes.9978332acf126e2c06e4126c6d4cbf38be593546: Hello again, friend. Your cure has worked wonderfully. Not a single citizen is infected any more. We're finally free of the plagues!
textByHashes.63b6d2fb4012c7c8a9fb8454240a1730ec6da9bf: We sent word to them earlier. They have agreed to meet with us at the bridge. If all goes well, this quarantine will soon be behind us.
textByHashes.f28ff5f4749f5f97df2a20ee1faa1557e897b8b0: Please do join us for the meeting. After all, none of this would have been possible without you.
textByHashes.e1fcdf2a5f498f7a2b04a531d5a6e17ab5ba8b51: Pharaoh Kemesis! I did not expect you to attend to this personally.
textByHashes.7eb1fe04ca16746cefb364ec62917ed218c653c6: Hello again! Please take this as a thank you for all your help.
textByHashes.1db30dd06d5c70748939f87d6ee995ed0629bbcf: Well now that we've sorted out this business with Klenter, we can properly focus on dealing with these plagues.
textByHashes.365f50fdce327f95face5adb7fcb611899eef526: Not great. However, we do have one bit of good news in that area, thanks to you.
textByHashes.4d5418e196945928862e4842db4ff0492231a34b: Better now that we've sorted out that business with Klenter. Still, we're no closer to dealing with these plagues.
textByHashes.46de353a57ac328e4be8a07083e62bb6cb464796: However, we do have one bit of good news in that area, thanks to you.
textByHashes.626c82dec60e382b22c6ee3a01ce936f261514b8: Yes. You've shown us that the pox we're all infected with can't be contagious. If it was, you'd be covered in spots by now.
textByHashes.f7e8706f60c50730ea93875b4cdd4b4be1332f77: It does indeed. The decision to lock things down was made very quickly, so we were forced to trap many of our citizens outside the walls. We'll be able to let them all back in now.
textByHashes.6f981b0ebc34ddaa6f722a51aff5e8ff9f70acb4: It is, but there is a problem.
textByHashes.ad2c10d30420a381d0a9109884728d6b8d51d56a: When the plague first started, Menaphos, our neighbouring city, closed their gates as well. Many of our citizens became trapped there when they did.
textByHashes.d6dcf8671e59e0db741cf93f300797c2b7b4ff00: You'd think so, but they believe that there is still a danger. It seems they're even willing to kill if it helps keep their city isolated.
textByHashes.a6cc61218f6d06b0b935c3f7f171e5a1a99bb1f2: The offer is appreciated, but I don't think there's anything you can do.
textByHashes.b433ac08f03c2f37e211bc9fab5a19c4f82d3779: You'd really be willing to help us again?
textByHashes.ff4f8c69d61811feaf05f7de88c5414a908a4b66: I'm very grateful. To be honest though, I'm not really sure where to start with this.
textByHashes.dbc23ca3a6c301ee0759f75943ba4d77e4f2e0e4: Indeed. Earlier today, I led a delegation across the bridge between the two cities in the hope of convincing them to end the quarantine. It didn't go well...
textByHashes.8cff711fd34c4430531a5679c7a84ebfcb795cf1: That is an improper greeting. You address the High Priest of Icthlarin!
textByHashes.c398cfbdada89365b1cec1876f36e48dc923aa7a: We have discovered that we are not contagious. You must let us pass!
textByHashes.568b1d804ddb4b38eb37a72fb58257096d664a9b: It is often the way under Pharoah Kemesis. He leads the Menaphites in a more secular manner than we would like. Respect for the old ways is diminishing.
textByHashes.b8aea392b0a2fe89e42026875c70861ecdde396c: The whole Menaphite Pantheon really. Religion is starting to mean less and less in Menaphos.
textByHashes.7c4bfc29aff589efab07311ed7288c96ebb94501: There was once a time when we would care for all of Menaphos' dead here. Now, many opt for less traditional burials.
textByHashes.34190f452a82461380ef0d97cfbdef42d5854065: Anyway, I've let myself become distracted. My focus is often on the dead, but right now our main worry is the living.
textByHashes.7fc4dbcd44bf22fc44887df2f00d082f5583f8dd: It's clear that the Pharaoh has no desire to open his city, so we need to find another way of getting our citizens home.
textByHashes.deca8e25c2f455b84919288263bce4998a8e534d: Well... I don't know. I'm open to any ideas you have.
textByHashes.a82546210efc40ac0b77c25f0a32458bf5417f2f: It would not work. Their quarantine is very strict, just as ours was. Even their own people are not allowed in or out of the city.
textByHashes.b34b32eee5a26e409e3886e0726fbdea93d67faa: The port south of the main city is still open. Traders take their goods to the city gates and exchange them with the guards there. Everything is thoroughly checked.
textByHashes.f6115eb4ce4538f084b3497bbae2dbd6906bd5f1: You'd want to swim across? If the current doesn't kill you, the crocodiles surely will.
textByHashes.fb7a1ce59598be771b3858c5e5c70d043d78b28f: Absolutely not! We here in Sophanem are still Menaphites and those guards are our brethren. Besides, I doubt you'd succeed against so many of them.
textByHashes.41c403a8821ce2f4dc6175096ad9e108fd24c63c: I don't know. I don't think so.
textByHashes.0b4e5f49c4caccbd85ede4054be3ab26622bdeaa: Beyond the homes of our dead? I can't imagine so. We are not ones for digging tunnels unnecessarily. We leave that nonsense to the Sect of Scabaras, and they're long gone.
textByHashes.88ac9968186a0ee8e2cc0fe41cc0b32d4931ce5d: They were a group dedicated to Scabaras, one of the lesser deities.
textByHashes.bc381a954fcdadcf2d1850ae131ec80be8f74453: You already know of Icthlarin and the Devourer. They are the son and daughter of Tumeken and Elidinis. Together, they are the four main deities of the Menaphite Pantheon.
textByHashes.4d00a9b4a1ad216487362683f8ebcc3d9734e546: However, the pantheon also has four lesser deities, or avatars as some call them. The Book of Light and Day says that Tumeken once fell into a deep sleep and dreamt for four days and four nights.
textByHashes.ee7a1b76f0189c3ddccdb03bb2cdaa31f160ed7f: With each night, his dreams took form. Those four forms became the lesser deities of the pantheon, with Scabaras being the last of them.
textByHashes.67e80d540c5158d06339fff3fc27f05924e1add9: That's right. Scabaras is the god of isolation. In keeping with his teachings, his followers mostly lived underground. As such, they were said to be great architects of the subterranean.
textByHashes.0d909df17010db6f8618c75ae18d0eba89caf5fc: The Devourer was not the only member of the pantheon to wish ill upon our people. It is said that many years ago, Scabaras and his sect tried to claim these lands as their own.
textByHashes.869db519258d736965754b26a3beddeff1566053: They built great tunnels beneath the Elid and used them to attack our settlements. However, Elidinis stood against them. The sect was destroyed, and Scabaras was fittingly driven into isolation.
textByHashes.20cb7cf7ef1d33861e64107f195319e8a4a978e4: Er... I suppose I've never considered it before. I guess it's not impossible.
textByHashes.73cbc12ec8963fcfdcf696dd5f0d55aedf7f5416: Maybe you should visit Jex at the Temple of the Lesser Gods in the north east corner of the city. He knows more about the Sect of Scabaras than I do.
textByHashes.b5bbabd9603f74d025b8ef4d34583682cf1ad0e3: In fact, now that I think about it, I recall a rumour that the temple was actually once home to the sect.
textByHashes.0650e8d3e28fd4acc5053de33453fce36860efac: Hello again. Have you had any thoughts on how we can get our citizens out of Menaphos?
textByHashes.d6f73bef769cd868e9fe6eacdd30df03fdfd8f21: Hello again. Did you visit Jex at the Temple of the Lesser Gods?
textByHashes.2e0c3fedfdab60c958f793af7cfcd8247fb7ba56: You'll find the temple in the north east corner of the city.
textByHashes.f8bf4bba9bf33a5240a39079b31c608d790cfd3a: Hello again. Any luck with getting our people out of Menaphos?
textByHashes.52f0a5f8170a4c1c79e5d96e291fc3f4a676c7b5: Well just let me know if I can help in any way.
textByHashes.2f0a084f3da5d508b653317217577db07d1e9514: Hello again. I have some wonderful news.
textByHashes.60dd3f8e514cc4352a5ab7f857316c21db3de5ff: Menaphos just sent over some of their dead. Imagine our suprise when we found some of our own citizens disguised among them! Apparently we have both you and someone called Maisa to thank.
textByHashes.09e930eca46d49e2c3f0c6656f72bd49b127386a: It seems that we are once again in your debt. Please, take this as a thank you.
textByHashes.a73c926b94e6d5d6e656317383ec1e287d4c5f2e: Many greetings. Welcome to our fair island.
textByHashes.9bac89f8683bcd251b22e86ba6f616e687e37119: A somewhat strange request, but I see no harm in it. There you go. May Saradomin walk with you.
textByHashes.065b530ea72b64c1fb718fb8e3b2b212f707fd77: A somewhat strange request, but I see no harm in... Wait a moment... is that BLOOD?
textByHashes.4e707df85cbcda3ec9a2910a1fd80cac442f86d3: Why do you want me to bless a pot full of someones blood???
textByHashes.62d760692783268d65084e662973cdd5428431cf: I... see... Well it is an unusual thing, but I see no harm in it. Take this, and walk in peace with Saradomin.
textByHashes.6db31ff71b64f0b88dfa732b7b3f2194c1947d16: The relic! This is a disaster!
textByHashes.f5dd014bae919cce84982231c6ee370821ceffbd: Adventurer, did you see who put that teleport beacon on the altar?
textByHashes.39c05c8ee0980137de2ec90910d7d0bf49744f7c: No worshipper of Saradomin would ever do something like this! Adventurer, you must return to Paterdomus and see if you can find any trace of this 'monk'.
textByHashes.7c7cc7f59c8e7fe9d8c0a7eaa69eb85f1d996087: Adventurer, have you found that 'monk' yet?
textByHashes.9600e92c25a0e167923f4473df2424a8645c1ca7: Please hurry to Paterdomus and see if you can find any trace of him.
textByHashes.64090e8ed9bb142ce4de815cda2eb6e331eddce4: This is not good. That relic wasn't just for show! It was once part of a powerful artefact. You must report this to Sir Tiffy Cashien in Falador Park.
textByHashes.1e2c71e6dd9c04c08c9529554433eca55a1bc920: Well adventurer, I have heard that they exist on this island, but have not ever seen one myself.
textByHashes.9a123424ac7349d30685318c78985dc5cf783c8e: According to legend however, they are so hot that their merest touch can burn flesh.
textByHashes.667307c4a19598a8428041363b16dc1f1049a595: Apparently long ago a great hero used a pair of magical gloves, stolen from the Queen of the Ice to cool the bird down enough to catch as a pet.
textByHashes.d845d0aecb9834d2d0611e0dc0dbac9da7fc8cba: I know not adventurer, it was just a myth I was told as a young boy. Perhaps somewhere icy?
textByHashes.298b1d5a71d5daa74f260740e5d94969c40d76a4: The object of which you speak did once pass through holy Entrana. I know not where it is now however.
textByHashes.465bd881204d27217f3b53120988f50c1d24f721: You! I will have no dealings with those who plunder the houses of the dead.
textByHashes.d0a9c5e0375b32d46671d0ce99b9795835b85882: Get thee gone from my presence!
textByHashes.e6ee744d9f76c4f14cf12cbcdfa2b4033019d2fc: The Sphinx? Is it possible? That contrary old cat has never done anything to help us. Prove it!
textByHashes.40a3b349172b758f5f0fc5082a9b9d5f68896887: Well then stop wasting my time! Can't you see I'm trying to put a stop to these plagues?
textByHashes.b8b9fda1fa955b7c78dec24ede4760255beefaa6: So what do you want from me, thief?
textByHashes.c60d185a08989772972f17cde46ca13a621183e1: Well you could have fooled me! Good people don't rob from the dead!
textByHashes.c20bf06fecb13cbdd9c3a34c2396ed0841f0eda6: This is all a disaster! The plagues were bad enough, but now we've got hypnotised adventurers plundering tombs! I've only just started this job as well!
textByHashes.a676471a212378c42d0fca8408f784a11ac82932: We fear they are a punishment sent by the gods. Perhaps we have displeased Icthlarin in some way. Maybe I am to blame. All this only started after I became High Priest.
textByHashes.35878669049ea6f2ee1cdfa9c9941dd01bf38e4c: Oh, er... I suppose the burial jar you took must be returned as soon as possible. That will allow my predecessor to rest once more.
textByHashes.55497a4530c6f2365c39196b0f1ec8478d03db09: I don't think so... No wait! There is something important.
textByHashes.adc453d8c24efd48e7c2889926fd2955ecdc461a: As with many of our tombs, the pyramid has magical seals to protect it from the Devourer, the goddess of destruction. The fact that you were able to get in before means that you must have a cat.
textByHashes.0732fb8d1d5501a2f8df24d477f2927ffa5e0fbb: Cats act as guardians against the Devourer. I'm surprised the Sphinx didn't tell you this. As such, only a cat can open the pyramid door.
textByHashes.48e2ad11666bd7e9708df21c818a46a72099dde7: One of my priests found this lying outside the pyramid. Please try to be more respectful of our dead.
textByHashes.eb08d38efdaa1d273823c2fecba64e6ba6bf6390: Have you managed to return the jar yet?
textByHashes.5efff42ae1725fe98ef1fc3d40af721b8298c4e7: Please hurry. The people are beginning to... well...
textByHashes.3997b776c2a3f371842432e30f9439c9981ff97c: No, just my authority. Don't forget that to enter the pyramid, you'll need a cat.
textByHashes.2e9d32b28facb62512d99ced9876ceaef17cde8b: Well those jars are a key part of our burial practices. To remove one is to directly insult not just Icthlarin, but all those that have passed on to the next life.
textByHashes.a50f7a10cb680baa5de8869714dc5a4c0050c7c6: Not to mention, it wasn't just any jar you took. You stole a jar from the tomb of our former High Priest! Why do you think his ghost is now wandering the city once more?
textByHashes.4cba9792fba8f329be0750df632c25b44038b7b8: Have you not noticed the plagues already filling our city? How long before Klenter calls upon Icthlarin to send a few more?
textByHashes.ef7d60c8e9c5f66895db09608210c46d5c7dad7a: For all we know, the plagues we already have could be punishment for your grave robbing!
textByHashes.ebe2e690a59181a6b621d93a882e3760ce2c4cfd: Perhaps Icthlarin foresaw what would happen and sent the plagues as a pre-emptive punishment?
textByHashes.857dc44c96066144b64a3274b51f3319b14e5e5d: I don't know... Icthlarin is a patient and forgiving god. It does not make sense to me that he would do this to us.
textByHashes.ce39d23f0affece4a457fcfe7c409e589c62594a: Perhaps. Either way, that jar needs to be returned.
textByHashes.486ad915b4e2a41f84e5b9b7a8d5000429f43534: They started a few days ago. We have locusts in the east of the city and frogs in the west. The cows have started to produce sour milk, and all of us have become infected with some sort of pox.
textByHashes.1be521c0f8ad711f8b91d50cf7a074268073896f: Once it became clear how bad it was, Menaphos, our neighbouring city, closed their gates to us and everyone else. We closed our own gates soon after.
textByHashes.097bae38aafa399bb3aa9fbe0f68420773c64a35: I didn't want to, but the other priests insisted. We had to stop the plagues from spreading. It seems that our choice was the right one. So far, the plagues have remained contained, but at a great cost.
textByHashes.282d483dcc8470aad5a32831cc4f58b79866e7ff: Many of our citizens are trapped outside the city. Some are stuck in Menaphos, with more in other parts of the desert.
textByHashes.30daba72e02963c0b072994772fe5095431a6195: However, that may well be for the best. Without trade from other settlements, we've been struggling to even feed the few of us left in the city.
textByHashes.a5e3941d088f141cfc6ad343d86b94eeb2425943: Not long. We need to find a way to stop the plagues soon.
textByHashes.8fd5b77240c15909a0ff49152f70112d6c80f83e: Icthlarin is the son of Tumeken and Elidinis and brother to the Devourer. He is the Menaphite god of the dead. He is responsible for ensuring the safe passage of all souls from this life to the next.
textByHashes.bf386acd67b00f57d84e1a5d0e2c23ca6da0a176: Here in Sophanem, we support Icthlarin in his role by making sure the dead are suitably prepared for their journey.
textByHashes.2309f49a3f7c063116b34b2accd53c0da59947c3: No. While Icthlarin is only worshipped here, his responsibilities stretch across all of Gielinor. He cares not for an individual's beliefs. All who pass on, enter his care.
textByHashes.fa015e583aa3140653f8e9c53b2842bb0a4b97ce: Which they are welcome to. It does not change the facts.
textByHashes.b03e70898a511218e8fe848769f8ca5ded3d71da: While most of the gods, Tumeken and Elidinis included, may be long gone from Gielinor, Icthlarin remains. His important role in caring for the dead means that he is still able to interact with us.
textByHashes.35d6b0e5dde4cc10725888422096a2b4ca958926: Well... no. While he is able to walk among us if he desires, he chooses not to so as to respect our way of life. Not to mention, his role requires his constant attention.
textByHashes.caed342e34021c8bc72691c3f055d93f402bbfe5: Long ago, back when Tumeken and Elidinis still walked these lands, they had two children. Icthlarin, god of the dead, was one. The other was Amascut, the goddess of rebirth.
textByHashes.94b5866d191c722599a7388483a7ce39da3719d9: For a time, Icthlarin and Amascut worked together in harmony. Icthlarin would ensure most souls are safely guided to the next life, while Amascut would select a small number to be reborn.
textByHashes.13fa2d18531e969ff448d8163ddb006799fe83e3: Yes. During a great war over these lands, Amascut became corrupted. She was warped into the Devourer, becoming the goddess of destruction.
textByHashes.fc9d61f7909dc9d0248d1c5cbb74e8074bdeae52: Since then, her only goal has been to devour every soul in existence, both living and dead.
textByHashes.19d1fb66cd3c8d18dbfb5f4542b0cc5e2ea1978c: We here have a lot of respect for the Sphinx. However, you should be careful of her words. She does not know the gods like we priests do.
textByHashes.b8fbaeec664f03a25b4f3554ab1d4aeac12633f3: Unfortunately so. Most gods have long since left this world. However, the Devourer was able to find a way to remain.
textByHashes.abb5e3dc446df9e9275ff94a845d44546f66259a: Luckily for us, some of her former priestesses were able to weaken her, but she still poses a great threat.
textByHashes.30ff8b05141bf9bfea536a9acd99440387c1156c: Wonderful! Now we can prepare for the ceremony.
textByHashes.e6694d145df7f480e6607a43d769585dead61465: The ceremony to reconsecrate Klenter's tomb of course!
textByHashes.c21bed060396fa1974b801c867f1ab61071615b8: Sorry, with all this plague business, it must have slipped my mind.
textByHashes.73be6ba971caba671674e3d9eb81dd76e5a08944: Well if you want to try running a city with multiple plagues, you're welcome to take over.
textByHashes.a2f765b4a10ecaca5cb85e983f092bdae605adb2: Good. Now, this ceremony is very dangerous, so we must ensure everything is done correctly.
textByHashes.6f0bb6d71c72513038fe181c9bf3cf05493b3daf: Well the pyramid has these magical seals on it...
textByHashes.ad828b59849e0c59eb5e7a3a2b2d88d28f1f339e: Right! Of course I did. Well for us to properly reconsecrate the tomb, we must first remove those seals. That means that once we begin, the pyramid will be vulnerable until the ceremony is complete.
textByHashes.2e2a0be3979d5150cb98303cf85ce282f185d04c: Now, we made many of the preparations while you were gone, but the carpenter and the embalmer could both use a hand. Could you go and speak with them?
textByHashes.d9eb88711828823ba3717b43af8298f12500cf5b: Did you forget that this is all your fault?
textByHashes.5901c96188614ce79c13fc16e0f2260e590cfd95: The job will be done when Klenter is at rest once more.
textByHashes.6306357d62493e5858996048160d2c58686c9987: Besides, thanks to the plagues, we're not allowing people to leave the city. However, as you seem to be unaffected, I've told the guards to make an exception for you.
textByHashes.b4b165122deb8143210ca91e58d1907ffbdbe562: That's right. We need the carpenter to create a new holy symbol for the ceremony. He lives over to the east. The embalmer also needs help gathering some supplies. He lives just south of here.
textByHashes.84447b7222517a872c654633c17d36f4a40d6f58: Have you helped the embalmer and the carpenter yet?
textByHashes.6e8d4c1c803b7ffc74cc8a4b12c2c368abe54d91: The embalmer lives just south of here and the carpenter lives over to the east. Please hurry. We need to get this ceremony performed before some other bad thing happens.
textByHashes.a8acda9b82a05826e5b583d7f4a1d88c2ea716ff: Ah there you are. Do you have the holy symbol?
textByHashes.2e888ad551f218e6ca274b4f03ff180847cf56bc: It can't be... It's the Devourer!
textByHashes.a08629a3eb0cde677cc514b7ed13590da9ba06d7: Protect us, adventurer! We must complete the ceremony!
textByHashes.fe9ef47ebae15220230af4f24ebddfa9efdc8552: Thank you so much, adventurer. Thanks to you, we were able to complete the ceremony.
textByHashes.0edbd65bd81d82bcf18e1d7200a5eea6215627cf: Well on that note, what exactly did happen there? That was the Devourer!
textByHashes.9c29d2ec993a250606f313fb76dc48f4d3e1c4e1: She wanted to kill us all in one go?
textByHashes.f587a3540887f3e3ab758b7c84d6f9911c58d6e5: Well once again, thank you. Without you, the Devourer would have struck a devastating blow against Icthlarin.
textByHashes.daab2a9b608ed01270de59944a59d9383f451e67: Thank you. We wouldn't have been able to complete the ceremony without that in our presence.
textByHashes.a3777a7563e5fc2d9c29149fb7e6f23efc5a2364: We have a few things to finish here, but then we'll head back up into the city. Please join us there.
textByHashes.dd6086d76160e6a3fd918c75bcc37390da7f8763: Ah, there you are. You were gone for quite a while. As a thank you for what you've done, I have a little trinket for you. I'm sure you'll get a huge amount of enjoyment out of it.
textByHashes.b682f777759291b725087006b58cce87623e7eff: Enjoy your stay here. May it be spiritually uplifting!
textByHashes.528e76392330e0bdd0c732599ee18473f9678658: Certainly. How much do you want?
textByHashes.2f4d9b4a05867219cf458cc1981dc480987b4d0e: Ah! A blessed sheet! This is perfect, I've been feeling rather cold the last few nights! As a token of my gratitude, I'm sure we can spare some paint for you.
textByHashes.3cba815a2a05f654ed1595ea2c03a5f1599c2b7e: If you'd like more paint, please ask for it. I'm sure we can spare you some.
textByHashes.8f5e7f7d476296007e2d8638892013f60603e683: How kind of you to bring me a message to this remote island!
textByHashes.b22ced375f8d64d0602f4e069af6a21383599c71: I don't have much in the way of wealth, but I can spare you a few coins for your trouble.
textByHashes.e6be91efd9369e51bfd2d1db6587895bf5f6ea33: In the name of Saradomin I shall bless you...
textByHashes.1463f9e609372e2234bb71fd051f7cc40cb7e5de: Here, let me give you some herbs.
textByHashes.88ecfb7a62b35dd949927e690e0eaeac9ee38be9: The beasts which dwell under this island occasionally drop gems when they die - please take this one as a sign of my gratitude.
textByHashes.e7eec4b5535819aabb8bb416a22a1e979cf7587f: No thank you, I am not accepting donations for the church at this time.
textByHashes.cda2511b842ad74dfab9b1c051d585a8fa41517d: Hello again. How can I help you?
textByHashes.60ec95b35fe77fa1c138dea189f4eb1dfc7e1bf2: Things are slowly coming back to life in our city of the dead. The bank under the Temple of the Lesser Gods has been restored, and a number of our merchants have reopened their stalls.
textByHashes.a97a74cab52b57418e655670a8e0616b8c07022b: We're making progress on them. With our citizens back, we had enough people to finally deal with the frogs and locusts that were filling the town up.
textByHashes.70694567a44f17511b9c537638aceb815d81cd17: The infected cows have been handled as well. The only plague left now is this infuriating pox. Still, at least it isn't contagious.
textByHashes.daa9210411037ab2e8dec20dd89d8cfb70399b42: They are adamant that their city must remain closed until all the plagues have gone. Until we can get rid of the pox, I can't see their gates opening to us, or to anyone else.
textByHashes.87e3397c7d4cc93284f98dd6699c8393dc5bab6d: Indeed. Now, is there anything else I can do for you?
textByHashes.a3966fbdeb778646100cdb33b429e37300401f1d: I'm afraid I only had the one.
textByHashes.decc868516ae9296f96eef3d3d868ecaf59fb3a0: Wonderful! The city of the dead has fully come back to life, and it's all thanks to you!
textByHashes.d99e96c47d1ee810b4b09063ebf246212ccb4f8b: They are continuing their preparations. It will take some time, but the city will soon be open once more.
textByHashes.a78e9493d8c67c4959bb5d8983eaf4d6382f4ff7: Certainly, though it will cost you 10,000 coins.
textByHashes.f0c94f9bcf40d68a67539df1d20e81af002770ac: You don't seem to have enough money for it.
textByHashes.94eb7454a7eb98482b32e391f50feb28de639fc6: Fair enough. Let me know if you change your mind.
textByHashes.09000d16e7bb45fa2b81d58dbe8d7279488d77f2: Hey, hey, hey! What in the name of sweet suffering Icthlarin are you doing?
textByHashes.1c7945ccc7f5a63c508243d4fb1a99824a9a52d6: All that you'll achieve by pouring that potion on me is to ruin my lovely robes.
textByHashes.f4022cc788fdc9631d5e98a49844f05dd6265bc7: Well, like you say, it is not really our way. Still, with all you've done for us, I'm willing to try.
